---
title: "Get data"
---

The fastest way to use BEAR is to download the combined `BEAR.rds`
artifact from [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/wwiecek/BEAR/releases).
Large data artifacts should live in releases rather than in this website
or in the repository history.

```r
bear <- readRDS("BEAR.rds")
```

From a shell, the current release artifact can be downloaded with:

```bash
curl -L -o BEAR.rds \
  https://github.com/wwiecek/BEAR/releases/download/v1/BEAR.rds
```

## Access individual datasets

If you want the individual processed datasets under `data/`, clone the
repository and initialise the data submodule:

```bash
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

In some cases the full dataset (e.g. millions of data points from PubMed scrape 
studies) is only available in the file in `data/`.
The repository intentionally keeps those individual datasets opt-in so that the 
main dataset remains small and easy to work with.
